description: Manifest describing the expected shape of sanitized production data samples used by the Go/Bun compatibility test harness (test/compat/production-data_test.go). This file documents what samples SHOULD contain; it does NOT contain or reference any real production data.
note: Do not commit real tokens, URLs, phone/device data, credentials, or media. Samples must be produced by scripts/ops/sanitize-db.ts before being supplied to the harness.

configFeatureFlags.description: Feature flag NAMES (booleans only) that may appear in the sanitized config JSON. Values are always boolean true/false in sanitized samples — never real URLs, tokens, or secrets.

versionLayouts.description: gowa_version values that may appear in samples. 'latest' is the default; explicit semver tags reflect pinned installs.

filesystemCategories.description: Expected directory structure under the data directory. Not all categories must be present in every sample, but the harness verifies the structure is not corrupted by Go startup.

rowCountRanges.description: Acceptable row-count ranges for the instances table in each sample. Ranges keep the harness fast while exercising realistic shapes.

samples.name: small-empty
samples.description: A freshly initialized database with zero instances. Validates that Go startup does not fail on an empty DB and that the schema is created idempotently.

samples.name: small-stopped
samples.description: A small set of stopped instances. Validates basic read paths and migration idempotency without side effects from process reconciliation.

samples.name: mixed-statuses
samples.description: A mix of stopped, running, and error instances. Validates that Go reconciliation handles each status without destructive mutation.

samples.name: large-deployed
samples.description: A larger production-like dataset to exercise read performance and ensure startup completes within reasonable time.
